brief_justification: Correctly identifies multiple comparisons as the exact core problem with sound reasoning; Goes three levels deep: inflated family-wise error rate, Bonferroni failure across all p-values, overlapping samples, and post-hoc segmentation; No explicit self-monitoring or bias-naming, proceeds linearly without questioning own framing; Explicit numerical verification: computes 1-(0.95)^3=14.3% and applies Bonferroni threshold 0.0167 to each p-value showing all fail; Acknowledges the Bonferroni correction is idealized and that the penalty is 'harder to compute' for correlated tests, but no structured confidence levels or what-would-change-conclusion analysis; Lists Bonferroni/Holm/BH alternatives and notes overlapping samples as a complication, but does not consider whether other statistical problems (e.g., p-hacking, SUTVA violations) might compete for 'most concerning'; Every conclusion has a derivation: formula, threshold, per-value check all shown explicitly; Avoids attributing conversion lift to correlated exogenous factors but does not name or explicitly reject that anti-pattern; Zero evidence of correlation-based attribution or shared-variance credit diffusion, and the framing is orthogonal to those failure modes; Response is linear despite the topology specifying a FORK→JOIN→gate structure for counterfactual simulation, no branching or gate logic visible
